skill.name: gws-gmail
skill.description: Send, read, and manage Gmail messages directly from the command line using Google Workspace CLI.
skill.summary: Send, read, and manage Gmail messages directly from the command line using Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The analyzer misidentified markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell execution and relative path references in documentation links as path traversal vulnerabilities. No actual security risks detected. This is an official Google Workspace skill that uses the `gws` CLI tool for Gmail operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Misidentification
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown inline code spans and code fences as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All detected 'external_commands' locations (lines 16, 18, 20, 26-32, 38-46, 52, 58, 60) are markdown code formatting syntax, not code execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations contain markdown backticks used for code formatting in documentation. The actual command examples (`gws gmail <resource> <method> [flags]`, `gws gmail --help`, `gws schema`) are legitimate CLI examples, not executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Path References Misidentified as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged relative path references like `../gws-gmail-send/SKILL.md` as path traversal sequences. These are standard markdown links to related skill documentation files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The `../` paths are markdown links referencing related skill files in the Google Workspace CLI repository structure. No actual filesystem operations or path traversal vulnerabilities exist in this SKILL.md file.

content.value_statement: Users need to send, read, and manage Gmail messages without leaving their terminal. This skill provides direct CLI access to Gmail operations through the official Google Workspace CLI tool.
actual_capabilities: Send emails with custom subject, body, recipients, and attachments
Read and display email content, headers, and thread information
Reply to or reply-all to existing email threads
List and manage inbox with unread message summaries
Stream real-time email notifications as NDJSON
limitations: Requires Google Workspace account with Gmail enabled
Needs initial authentication via `gws auth` command
Complex email threading and scheduling features not supported

use_cases.title: Automate Email Notifications
use_cases.description: Send automated status updates, alerts, or reports to stakeholders directly from CI/CD pipelines or scripts.

use_cases.title: Quick Email Management
use_cases.description: Read and triage inbox messages without opening a browser, ideal for power users who prefer terminal workflows.

use_cases.title: Email Workflow Integration
use_cases.description: Incorporate email sending into Claude Code workflows for automated customer communications or notifications.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Simple Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an email to [recipient] with subject [subject] and body [message] using the gws-gmail skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to send a straightforward email message

prompt_templates.title: Read Recent Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my recent unread emails in Gmail using the gws-gmail skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to check inbox without opening email client

prompt_templates.title: Reply to Email Thread
prompt_templates.prompt: Reply to the most recent email from [sender] with: [your response]. Use the gws-gmail skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to quickly respond to a message

prompt_templates.title: Automated Email Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create and send a daily summary report to [team-email] containing: [report summary]. Use gws-gmail to send the email.

output_examples.input: Send an email to team@example.com with subject 'Deployment Complete' and body 'The production deployment finished successfully.'
output_examples.output:

Message ID: abc123xyz
Thread ID: thread456abc

3 unread messages:

   Subject: Invoice #12345
   Date: Today 9:15 AM

3. From: alerts@monitoring.io

output_examples.input: Reply to the email from manager@company.com with 'I will review the document and send feedback by end of day.'
output_examples.output:
Reply sent successfully.
Original thread preserved.
Message added to existing conversation.

best_practices: Always verify recipient addresses before sending to prevent misdirected emails
Use descriptive subjects that clearly indicate email purpose
Test email sending in dry-run mode if available before automated deployments
anti_patterns: Do not embed sensitive credentials directly in email body
Avoid sending large attachments via CLI; use cloud storage links instead
Do not spam recipients with automated messages without proper throttling

faq.question: Do I need a Google Workspace account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Google account with Gmail enabled. Google Workspace accounts work out of the box, and personal Gmail accounts can be used with additional configuration.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Gmail?
faq.answer: Run 'gws auth login' or 'gws auth' to authenticate with your Google account. Follow the browser-based OAuth flow to grant permissions.

faq.question: Can I send attachments with emails?
faq.answer: Yes, use the gws-gmail-send subcommand with the appropriate attachment flags to send files along with your message.

faq.question: How do I view email threads instead of individual messages?
faq.answer: Use the threads resource with the gws-gmail command. Check 'gws schema gmail.threads.list' for available options.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many emails I can send?
faq.answer: Gmail has built-in sending limits (about 500 recipients per day for regular accounts, higher for Workspace). Check Google support for current limits.

faq.question: Can this skill access all my Gmail labels and folders?
faq.answer: Yes, the Gmail API supports full label management. Use the labels resource to list, create, or modify labels on your messages.
